summary: Configure automatic Docker image build in loanflow-parent via spring-boot-maven-plugin
description:
Wire the `spring-boot-maven-plugin` `build-image` goal into the `package` lifecycle phase inside <loanflow-parent/pom.xml> so that every child Spring Boot service produces a Docker image automatically when `./mvnw package` is run.

Two top-level properties are defined in the parent `<properties>` block:

```
<image.repository>loanflow</image.repository>
<image.name>${image.repository}/${project.artifactId}:${project.version}</image.name>
```

`image.repository` acts as a placeholder for the Docker registry or ECR repository prefix and can be overridden at build time (e.g. by a Jenkins pipeline via `-Dimage.repository=123456789.dkr.ecr.eu-west-1.amazonaws.com/loanflow`) without touching the POM files.

`image.name` composes the full image reference from these parts and can be further overridden per child service if a non-standard naming convention is required.

No child service should need to redeclare the `build-image` execution — it is inherited from the parent plugin management block.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Default image name is derived from repository prefix and artifactId
acceptanceCriteria.given: a child service does not override image.repository or image.name
acceptanceCriteria.when: ./mvnw package is run for that service
acceptanceCriteria.then: a Docker image is produced with the name loanflow/<artifactId>:<version>

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: image.repository can be overridden at build time
acceptanceCriteria.when: ./mvnw package is executed for any child service
acceptanceCriteria.then: the produced Docker image is tagged as 123456789.dkr.ecr.eu-west-1.amazonaws.com/loanflow/<artifactId>:<version>

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Child service can override image.name entirely
acceptanceCriteria.given: a child service declares <image.name>myregistry.io/custom-name:latest</image.name> in its properties block
acceptanceCriteria.when: ./mvnw package is run for that service
acceptanceCriteria.then: the Docker image is tagged as myregistry.io/custom-name:latest, ignoring the parent default

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: build-image goal is bound to package phase
acceptanceCriteria.given: the loanflow-parent pluginManagement configures build-image in the package phase
acceptanceCriteria.when: any child Spring Boot service runs ./mvnw package
acceptanceCriteria.then: the build-image goal executes as part of the standard Maven lifecycle without requiring an explicit goal invocation

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: loanflow-common is excluded from image build
acceptanceCriteria.given: loanflow-common is a library module with no Spring Boot main class
acceptanceCriteria.when: the parent or multi-module build runs
acceptanceCriteria.then: loanflow-common does not attempt to build a Docker image and the build succeeds cleanly

tasks.summary: Add image.repository and image.name properties to loanflow-parent pom
tasks.description:
In <loanflow-parent/pom.xml>, add the following inside the `<properties>` block:

```
<image.repository>loanflow</image.repository>
<image.name>${image.repository}/${project.artifactId}:${project.version}</image.name>
```

`image.repository` defaults to `loanflow` for local development and can be replaced by a CI/CD pipeline (e.g. an ECR URI) via a `-D` flag without modifying any POM file.

tasks.summary: Configure spring-boot-maven-plugin build-image in parent pluginManagement
tasks.description:
Inside the `<build><pluginManagement><plugins>` section of <loanflow-parent/pom.xml>, configure the `spring-boot-maven-plugin` to bind the `build-image` goal to the `package` phase and reference the `${image.name}` property:

```
<plugin>
  <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
  <artifactId>spring-boot-maven-plugin</artifactId>
  <executions>
    <execution>
      <id>build-image</id>
      <phase>package</phase>
      <goals>
        <goal>build-image</goal>
      </goals>
      <configuration>
        <image>
          <name>${image.name}</name>
        </image>
      </configuration>
    </execution>
  </executions>
</plugin>
```

tasks.summary: Verify image.repository override in loan-service child pom
tasks.description:
Run a local build of <loan-service> passing the image.repository override to confirm the inheritance and property substitution chain works end-to-end:

```
./mvnw package -pl backend/loan-service -Dimage.repository=myregistry.io/loanflow -DskipTests
```

Confirm `docker images` shows the correctly tagged image after the build completes.

In <loanflow-common/pom.xml>, add a skip configuration for the `spring-boot-maven-plugin` so the library module does not attempt to produce a Docker image (it has no Spring Boot main class):

```
<plugin>
  <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
  <artifactId>spring-boot-maven-plugin</artifactId>
  <configuration>
    <skip>true</skip>
  </configuration>
</plugin>
```

tasks.summary: Document image.repository CI/CD override in README or docs
tasks.description: Add a short note to <docs/roadmap.md> or <README.md> explaining the two properties and how a Jenkins pipeline should pass `-Dimage.repository=<ECR_URI>` during the package phase to produce correctly tagged ECR images without POM changes.
